title: auganthus mollis
side_effects: Auganthus mollis can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of toxic compounds like alkaloids and glycosides. Prolonged exposure to the plant's extracts can also lead to liver damage and increased risk of bleeding, as it interferes with the body's clotting mechanisms. In some cases, excessive consumption of Auganthus mollis can cause allergic reactions, such as skin rashes, itching, and respiratory issues. If ingested in large quantities or used as a medicinal treatment without proper guidance, Auganthus mollis can have severe and potentially life-threatening consequences.
